ddls:
CREATE TABLE "pins" (
  "pin_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pin */,
  "pin_name" TEXT /* Name of the pin (e.g., PA1, PA2) */,
  "function" TEXT /* Function of the pin (e.g., RMII Reference Clock) */,
  "conflict_with" TEXT /* Conflict with other connectors or signals */,
  "pin_type" TEXT /* Type of pin (e.g., GPIO, ADC, UART) */,
  "max_voltage" REAL /* Maximum voltage the pin can handle */,
  "min_voltage" REAL /* Minimum voltage the pin can handle */,
  "current_rating" REAL /* Maximum current the pin can handle */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the pin (e.g., active, inactive, reserved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pin_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration record */,
  "pin_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the pin associated with this configuration */,
  "connector_type" TEXT /* Type of connector in use (e.g., Ethernet, ST Zio) */,
  "configuration_when_eh" TEXT /* Configuration when using Ethernet */,
  "configuration_when_st" TEXT /* Configuration when using ST Zio or ST morpho connector */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version of the configuration */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the configuration */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the configuration */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was last modified */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_pin_id FOREIGN KEY ("pin_id") REFERENCES pins ("pin_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "pin_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pin that was changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., update, delete) */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration that was changed */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version of the configuration at the time of the change */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* The old value before the change */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* The new value after the change */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_pin_id FOREIGN KEY ("pin_id") REFERENCES pins ("pin_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_configuration_id FOREIGN KEY ("configuration_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "conflicts" (
  "conflict_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each conflict record */,
  "pin_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pin with the conflict */,
  "conflicting_function" TEXT /* Function that causes the conflict */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the conflict */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the conflict record */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the conflict record was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the conflict record */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the conflict record was last modified */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the conflict resolution (e.g., open, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conflict_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_conflicts_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_conflicts_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_conflicts_pin_id FOREIGN KEY ("pin_id") REFERENCES pins ("pin_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version record */,
  "pin_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pin associated with this version */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date of the version release */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the version */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was last modified */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if this is the current version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_pin_id FOREIGN KEY ("pin_id") REFERENCES pins ("pin_id")
)
